fields.name: Allergies: every person, every severity (food, medication, environmental, insect)
Medical alerts and devices (EpiPen/inhaler/other): locations, expiration check cadence (VT)
Security or legal flags: NDA, custody arrangements, restraining orders, do-not-admit list
Household summary paragraph (drafted after intake, client-readable)
Adult 1: full name, preferred name and never-call-me
Adult 1: mobile, email, and which is REAL for urgent vs routine
Adult 1: contact hours, response pattern, communication style
Adult 2: same four rows
Other adults in residence: same
Decision-maker map: who decides what; where deference runs
Contact priority order, and who must never be contacted about what
Emergency contacts beyond residents (name, relation, when to use)
Household mailing list: where it lives, who maintains it (feeds the card and announcement programs, WK_02Q)
Important-dates registry: birthdays (family and close extended), anniversaries, recurring occasions; teacher-gift and host-gift moments (feeds gift and card programs, WK_02Q)
Names: correct pronunciation (phonetic), honorifics, and the names each person actually goes by vs never
Open ask, verbatim: "Are there practices, traditions, or rules of the house we should know to serve you respectfully?" (the door to everything below)
Employer / role fields (for the stress calendar and, corporate-only, the SOFTEN alert layer; public news never mentioned to the client)

CADENCE REGISTRY (per adult; feeds WK-APP-002 PM domain) [S2]: dental (last cleaning), physical (last annual), vision (last exam / lens supply), prescriptions (refill cycles: date+pharmacy only, never clinical detail), grooming cadence, passport/license expiry, vehicle registration/inspection/service, memberships and auto-renewals with keep/cancel dates

PARTNER DISCRETION DEFAULT (single-client households) [S2]: new-presence protocol is say nothing / adjust nothing / assume nothing until directed; partner-preferences lite intake only on client direction; privacy at maximum

Firearms in the home: presence, storage method (secured/unsecured; safe, lockbox, or cabinet), ammunition stored separately? CRITICAL if unsecured and children (resident or regular visitors) have access

Medications and controlled substances: secure-storage status (locked cabinet or bag) where controlled substances are present and the HM has regular unsupervised access; companion to the S2 prescription cadence row above, never adding clinical detail
Cannabis and alcohol: home cultivation or bar/cellar storage secured from minors and visiting children; state legal limits noted where home cultivation applies

Security clearance, sensitive federal occupation, or multiple, diplomatic, or international-organization passport situation: CRITICAL-flag-eligible; client marks that heightened discretion applies without disclosing agency or clearance level

Gift-flagging protocol: an unusual or high-value gift, particularly from a foreign source (2026 federal minimal-value threshold: $525), is flagged for the client's own review; Well Kept and the HM never assess or advise, only flag and report, the same standard as the DO-NOT-ADMIT register
Formal or official forms of address (title, honorific, military rank) where correspondence or hosting requires it, distinct from the everyday never-call-me preference
Language(s) spoken at home and by whom: an HM's own language capability as a matching consideration, and any language-development practice (such as an immersion rule) the HM is asked to respect

Occupation-driven scheduling pattern, where relevant: on-call/shift unpredictability (medicine, with post-shift quiet protection), inverted hours (chefs/hospitality, evening peak), predictably-unpredictable intensity cycles (legislative session, trial, closing deadline: hard shift for a defined window, then returns to normal), bid/rotation schedule (flight crew, knowable weeks out if asked), reserve/National Guard duty (periodic drill weekends and annual training, plus possible unpredictable activation), rotating shift work (nursing, law enforcement, utilities: quiet-time and presence windows cycle month to month rather than staying fixed), or bimodal absence work (touring performers, wildland firefighters, campaign staff: alternates between near-total absence and full presence); for a rotating-shift household, childcare-handoff coverage is cross-referenced to Section 3, since who is available for pickup shifts with the rotation — named examples, not exhaustive
Each child: name, age, school and grade, weekly schedule
Each child: room rules and boundaries (enter? knock? never?)
Each child: what they notice, need, or are proud of (the duvet layer)
Child-related rules: screens, snacks, homework, what HM may/may not do
Other dependents (elder care, special needs): needs and boundaries
Each pet: species, name, age, temperament with strangers
Feeding: what, when, where, who; treats policy
Door, gate, and room rules; escape risks (CAUTION candidates)
Vet: practice, emergency vet, medications, insurance
Walker/sitter arrangements and access
Every regular presence: au pair, housekeeper, nanny, walker, trainer, cleaner: name, days/hours
Each: scope, and the boundary with HM scope (name the overlap and the gaps)
Each: coordination route (through client? direct? never?)
Regular deliveries and services: what arrives when, and what to do with it
Sizes registry per child: clothing, shoe, uniform sizes, updated at seasonal changeovers (required by Back-to-School and gift bundles, WK_02Q)
School communication channels: which portal/app/email supply lists and forms arrive through; forwarding or access arrangement for the HM
House rules the HM must never undermine: screens, snacks, chores; WHOSE JOBS ARE THE CHILDREN'S OWN (what is deliberately left undone for them)
Information boundaries [S2]: tooth fairy / Santa protocols; anything the children do not know; who may be told what
Discipline / redirection method: how parents want behavior handled, AND the firm boundary that the HM never disciplines (only gently redirects and reports)

CADENCE REGISTRY, children [S2]: well-child visit (birthday-adjacent), dental, vision, vaccination/school-form deadlines, sizing check (6 wk pre-season), activity gear and registration windows, passport 5-year marks
CADENCE REGISTRY, pets: vet annual, vaccines, license renewal, flea/heartworm supply cycle, grooming cadence

CUSTODY SCHEDULE (if applicable) [S2]: which weeks/days each child is home (drives visit rhythm and briefing); co-parent handoff logistics (who, where, what travels with the child); INFORMATION BOUNDARY with co-parent (what the HM may confirm: default nothing without direction; neutrality applies doubly); duplicate-gear map across two homes

FULL RESIDENT MAP beyond nuclear family: resident grandparent/extended family (own cadences, aides, privacy standards); part-time residents (boomerang adult children, college breaks, exchange students: their rhythm and space); live-in staff room/board boundaries; tenant or ADU occupant (present, never served: access + legal line)

REGULAR VISITOR ROSTER: playdate/carpool children (names + ALLERGY CROSS-CHECK vs S1, mandatory); in-home service visitors interacting with children (tutors, therapists, trainers, clergy: screening/supervision expectations); neighbor-with-key; renovation-period protocol trigger

FOSTER / KINSHIP / TEMPORARY GUARDIANSHIP [S2]: placement-ready mode (room + age-ranged essentials kit maintained; dignity-first arrival kit: new items, real bag); licensing compliance cadence (locked meds, water-heater temp, detectors, firearms storage, egress per state standards; inspection-ready always); caseworker visit protocol (credentials verified); visitation/exchange logistics under agency rules

FOSTER CONFIDENTIALITY (LAW, not courtesy) [S3-adjacent]: NO photographs of foster children ever; no names/case details in any record beyond first name; media logic default-never; HM discusses placements with no one; trauma-informed notes (snack basket stocked + reachable; never comment on food taken/kept; predictable rhythms)

ALL-SPECIES PET CAPTURE: BIRDS: bird-safe product list is CRITICAL-FLAG (no aerosols, scented candles, plug-ins; nonstick cookware rules), cross-file to S1+S13. TANKS/REPTILES: life-support outlets on never-switch-off list; feeding protocol (frozen/live food in freezer inventory); specialist service vendor; POWER-OUTAGE RESPONSE PLAN (battery/generator priority, temperature windows). Small mammals: cage cadence, chew hazards

HORSES (boarded or on-property): barn contacts + family visit rhythm; if on-property: farrier + vet cadences, hay/feed ordering, trough de-icing, barn/paddock in sentinel sweep. BACKYARD FLOCK: coop cadence, predator check in sentinel, egg handling, winter water
HM ASSIGNMENT MATCH: any HM allergy / fear / comfort limit by species (disclosed at hiring, matched at assignment, never discovered in the home)
Property: type, size, floors, rooms count; layout sketch or photo set
Exterior zones: storage, sheds, pools, play structures; codes to app
Parking: where HM parks, gate/HOA rules
Waste: trash/recycling/compost cadence, bin locations, roll-out and return days
Every entry: method, hardware, and the REAL hands-full entry (VT)
Keys: every key that exists, who holds copies (WK-SOP-015 log)
Alarm: system, modes, quirks and drift doors (VT); codes to app secure field
Garage and gate: openers, codes (app), interior-door alarm behavior (VT)
Cameras: every location, coverage, recording reality (assume always-on)
No-photo zones and topics; media-release status (signed/declined/partial per WK-LEG-002)
Rooms or subjects the client marks private (off-limits is a confirmed fact)
Main water shutoff: location, handle type (VT)
Gas shutoff and smell-gas protocol (VT)
Electrical panel: location, labeled? known trips (VT)
Extinguishers, smoke/CO detectors: locations, test dates (VT)
First aid and medical devices: locations (cross-flag S1 CRITICAL) (VT)
Evacuation notes and utility emergency numbers
Pool / spa: present? current service arrangement
Generator / backup power: presence, fuel type and supply, transfer-switch location, and test-run schedule, service history
Vehicles: count, parking, detailing or service preferences
Mail and packages protocol: where deliveries go, what may be opened (default: nothing), signature items, porch habits
Returns protocol: where returns pile, who initiates, which carriers/drop-offs the household uses (a classic friction point; often becomes a standing HM task)
HOA / neighborhood context: bin rules, contractor hours, parking rules, quiet hours, the neighbor relationships that matter (the shared fence, the noise-sensitive side)
Shoes-off household? HM practice (dedicated indoor shoes / covers); threshold customs
Disaster-preparedness plan: emergency-kit location, meeting point, pet-evacuation plan, reach-us tree; regional exposures (derecho, hurricane remnants, winter power loss, flooding)
Door-answering policy: does the HM answer the door? For whom (known vendors only)? How are vendor visits pre-confirmed? Unexpected-caller protocol (caregiving-industry safety standard)
DO-NOT-ADMIT REGISTER [S2/S3]: estranged persons, former staff, anyone under active legal order; the response script (decline warmly, report immediately, never adjudicate)
NEIGHBOR RELATIONS MAP: adjacent households by name; the friendly one / the sensitive one; dispute history; shared driveways, fences, easements; the neighbor who takes packages

HOA / CIVIC ASSOCIATION working detail: bin out/in rules + fine triggers; holiday-decor up/down deadlines; architectural approval requirements + lead time; lawn standards; vendor access + insurance cert rules; amenities; association calendar

BUILDING LOGISTICS (condo/townhome): front desk contacts by name; freight elevator reservation rules; loading dock; package room; quiet hours; building mgmt contact; building-held master shutoffs; staff holiday tipping custom (feed to person registry)
PARKING: where the HM parks; where vendors park; guest passes; permit zones; street-sweeping days; snow-emergency rules
NEIGHBORHOOD TEXTURE: bus stop + times; mail/package patterns + porch-piracy risk; leaf collection + yard-waste windows; community listserv; wildlife pressure; block events
Firearms storage: location type (safe, lockbox, cabinet) and who holds the combination or key; the combination itself is never written here, entered only in the app secure field per WK-SOP-015
Firearms: standing safety-inventory item alongside extinguishers and detectors; ammunition storage location if kept separate from the firearm
Hazardous chemical storage: pool or spa chemicals, pesticides and fertilizers, propane tanks, automotive fluids: location and security, especially where children or pets have access
Pool / spa safety protocol: fencing present? self-latching gate? pool alarm? supervision rule while the HM is present; the same rigor as any child- or pet-adjacent hazard
Hot tub / sauna: present? heat-safety and supervision notes, same protocol as the pool where children or pets have access
Vehicle logistics: which key or fob belongs to which vehicle, where each is parked or garaged, spare key location, and which vehicle (if any) has a car seat installed
Safe deposit box: bank, branch, authorized signers, key location (never stored with the box number in one unsecured place)
Offsite storage unit: facility, unit number, gate code, lock combination, key location, and a basic contents inventory
Shed or other detached structure: contents; cross-reference to Section 9 if it stores fuel, pesticides, or power equipment
Crawl space, where present: is HM or assessor entry authorized at all, given fall, air-quality, and pest risks distinct from an attic or basement; if not, recorded as off-limits, not scored
Flag protocol, where a flag is flown: illumination after dark or retrieval before storms, weather-resistant material, retirement of a worn flag, awareness of half-staff notification days

Classified or sensitive government workspace: never photographed, never catalogued in the app, never included in the appliance or organization registries, a stricter standard than an ordinary private room
Food safety during an extended outage: refrigerator and freezer contents, how long is safe to hold, what gets discarded, chest freezer or cooler-with-ice fallback
Sump pump: present? battery backup confirmed present or absent (flagged specifically since the same storm that causes an outage often causes the flooding the pump prevents)
Backup communication if cell service is affected: landline, neighbor's contact, or another agreed method
HVAC: zones, thermostats, settings, do-not-adjust rules; filter sizes and cadence
Water heater: type, location, age, quirks
Electrical quirks: the outlet that trips, the switch nobody knows
Internet: provider, router location, restart ritual; network names (passwords to app)
Smart home: platform, devices, who controls, what HM touches
Kitchen appliances: each nameplate photographed; brand/model/quirks
Laundry: washer/dryer models, settings that work, lint discipline
Filters registry: every filter in the home (HVAC, fridge, water, vacuum) size + cadence
Manuals: where they live (or photo library)
Service history and warranties: what is under contract with whom; coverage window and claim contact logged per item, not just presence of a contract
High-value items registry: art, wine collection, instruments, valuables requiring specialist handling (S2; feeds WK_05H briefs)
Household digital stack: family calendar system (Google/Cozi/other), shared lists apps, and which the HM works within for carpool/activity calendars
Subscriptions and memberships inventory: streaming, boxes, Prime/Costco, delivery memberships (feeds the Annual Vendor Audit, WK_02Q)
Account access boundaries: which household accounts the HM may order through, with what limits (ties to spending authority and WK_06E)

Equipment used in service: client-owned vacuum (model, bag/filter size), mop system, other tools; consumable sizes and where spares live (cleaning-industry practice: never arrive to a full vacuum bag)

LIFECYCLE DATES: install/manufacture year per major item (from nameplates): water heater, roof, HVAC, washer hoses, sump, disposal; feeds the pre-failure replacement horizons (replace on schedule beats replace in crisis) — manufacturer-recommended intervals as a reference: water heater 8-12 yrs, HVAC 15-20 yrs, smoke/CO detector units 10 yrs, roof by material's rated life. Extends to safety-critical and frequently-restocked supplies, not only fixed systems: cleaning products (6mo-2yr; bleach specifically degrades within 6 months even sealed), stored batteries (per package; smoke/CO detector batteries at least annually regardless of remaining charge), paint (6mo-5yr opened), fire extinguishers (inspected annually; recharge/replacement interval confirmed against the specific unit's own manufacture date, varies by type)
EV charger, home battery, or solar system: location, breaker or disconnect, charging-cable handling (never unplug mid-cycle), installer/service contact; same logic as the generator entry above
Basic tool inventory (ladder, drill, hand tools): what exists, where it lives, condition; distinct from the workshop power-equipment safety boundary
Specialty appliances and gadgets easy to overlook or mishandle (water softener, ice maker, wine fridge, robot vacuum, and similar): what exists, care cadence, manual location

STORED VALUE REGISTRY: every gift card, prepaid or stored-value account (EZ-Pass and similar), paid subscription, and use-it-or-lose-it class package or membership credit, logged with current balance or remaining uses and expiration/renewal date where one exists — the digital stack (row above) made active, not merely inventoried
Dietary: allergies (cross-flag S1), restrictions, strong dislikes
Staples list: the always-in-stock items, brands non-negotiable vs flexible
Grocery: stores, order-vs-shop, budget rhythm, substitution rules
Pantry and fridge logic: zones, rotation, leftovers policy
Meal rhythms: who cooks, when, what HM preps or never touches
Products by surface: floors, counters, glass, bath; storage locations
The do-not-use list AND WHY (grout, finishes, scent sensitivities) (CAUTION)
Scent policy: loved, tolerated, banned
Laundry scope: whose clothes are IN, whose are NEVER (CAUTION)
Products, temperatures, air-dry list, fold standards per item class
Linen rotation: sets per bed, change cadence, where spares live
Special care: the items that have a story (heirloom quilt, the jersey)
The home's logic: what lives where and the why behind it
Container and label system in place (WK-SVC-003 palette) or to be built
Homeless-item protocol: where unsorted things go, and the never-discard rule
Donation/discard authority: what requires written approval (per WK-LEG-001: everything)
Delivery and meal services in use: grocery delivery, meal kits, prepared meals, restaurant standbys; who manages each account
Preferred retailers by category: kids' clothes, home goods, pharmacy, hardware; the loyalty accounts that matter
The small standing orders: the coffee order, the bakery item, the Friday tradition (DELIGHT candidates)
Dietary law where kept: kosher separation (which dishes/utensils/zones), halal sourcing, faith-based food days; fasting periods and their meal rhythms (suhoor/iftar)
Sacred objects & spaces protocol: what exists (mezuzah, altar/shrine, prayer rugs, texts), placement/orientation rules, what may be dusted, what is NEVER touched (CAUTION rows, per object)
Cultural cookware on the never-soap list: seasoned wok, cast iron, clay pots, tea items
GEAR ZONES: where each hobby/fandom kit lives (tailgate rig, golf bag, jerseys, cooler, stadium clear bag, ski gear); condition and readiness owner; pre-season refresh window
Auxiliary freezer or bulk food storage outside the kitchen (garage, basement): inventory logic, rotation and labeling, distinct from the kitchen pantry
Uniform or duty-gear laundering held to shift-turnaround urgency rather than standard cadence (scrubs, a uniform, chef whites): ready before the next shift, not eventually
Rapid-turnaround dry cleaning for a high-visibility public role, where wardrobe readiness cannot wait for the normal cycle
Meal timing and grocery-delivery windows built around an inverted or rotating schedule rather than a standard day (a night-shift household's “dinner” may be at 6am)
EVERY room, one row each (add rows): owner, standard, boundaries, quirks; off-limits recorded as such
Garage / basement / attic / closets: included, never skipped
(Built from all sections) Visit sequence draft: arrival ritual, order, timing, departure checklist (lock, arm, log)
Corrections observed: what the family moved back after service (the correction IS the preference)
Consumption reality: what runs out faster than the staples list predicts
Friction points: the chronic snags the family stopped naming (bins, backpacks, the drifting door)
Rhythm map: the household week and year as actually lived
Every "oh, that is just how we do it" logged verbatim, dated
DOT LOG: things heard in passing (offhand wishes, cravings, traditions mentioned once, store complaints) logged verbatim the day heard; the raw material for gestures
GESTURE LOG: what was done, when, sourced from which dot, how it landed; never repeat until stale

CONDITION BASELINE: pre-existing damage and wear per room, photographed at onboarding (scratched floor, chipped tile, stained ceiling corner); anchors any later damage conversation; both parties protected

SENTINEL SWEEP (every visit, in the final walkthrough): under-sink and toilet-base moisture, ceiling/wall water stains, pest evidence, unusual appliance/HVAC sounds, unfamiliar smells, detector glance; abnormalities photographed and reported same day

LOAD SIGNALS (trend, not judgment): arrival-state trend across visits; takeout packaging, laundry backlog, unopened deliveries, mail pile, plants missed; three-week trend fires the surge-care response, said to no one

Transportation safety protocol (if transporting child/client): no phone/texting while driving, hands-free only, speed limits, car-seat/booster config by age, no unauthorized passengers, documented routes, who may ride when

SENSORY ARRIVAL STANDARD (per room / whole home): what it should FEEL like on entry, light (blinds/lamps set how), scent (house scent or none; where fragrance is unwelcome), sound (music off? a quiet?), temperature, the order that reads as "done"
THE ENGINEERED HOMECOMING: what the client SEES on walking in (cleared island, lit lamp, note, flowers) and the final touch before departure, first and last moments designed, not incidental

FANDOM & HOBBY REGISTRY: teams and artists followed (and colors never to get wrong); season tickets/memberships (seats, account, renewal date); per-adult hobby roster (golf, tennis, running, boating, collecting) with season, cadence, gear home

FINE-FINISH REGISTRY (child-free households especially): the materials family homes cannot keep (art glass at reach height, white upholstery, honed marble, silk, open shelving) with specialized care cadences: sealing schedule, conditioning, gallery-grade never-use list
ROOMS-SERVE-PURSUITS MAP: which spaces serve which adult passion (cellar, studio, library, gym, workshop); the standard of done per room set to the pursuit, not a generic reset

COLLECTION REGISTRY: art, watches, instruments, wine cellar (with DRINKING WINDOWS per bottle/case where the client tracks them); acquisition protocol (photo, insurance rider/appraisal route, placement per fine-finish rules, care cadence)

HOBBY-CARE REGISTRY: LIVING ASSETS (starter feeding cadence, orchids/plants, garden + frost dates, hives): joins AWAY-MODE dependency list (they die during travel). HUMIDITY-SENSITIVE (piano 42-45% + tuning 2x/yr, string instruments + case humidifiers in heating season, humidor, vinyl vertical, optics): humidity map + winter cadence. DEGRADATION/SAFETY (classic car battery tender + exercise runs, ski edges/wax, leather conditioning, climbing rope + helmet RETIREMENT DATES: hard dates, never judgment calls). Equipment servicing (kiln, sewing machine, espresso backflush) per appliance logic; textiles: moth-season inspection

Workshop or power-equipment safety boundary: table saw, welding gear, soldering station or similar: never operate, never move mid-project; safe-to-clean-around state confirmed each visit, the same never-touched logic Section 13 gives sacred objects

Burn/fire-risk equipment note (kiln, 3D printer, soldering station, espresso machine): hot-when-running state, ventilation needs, whether ever mid-cycle during a visit; companion to the equipment-servicing cadence above

Household goods provenance: personally owned versus employer-provided or temporary-housing furniture, and anything already earmarked for storage or shipment; a piece that is not the family's own or is bound for a container carries a different handling standard than an heirloom

Day-sleeper room readiness (rotating shift, on-call, or overnight work): blackout coverage that functions, temperature held for daytime sleep, do-not-disturb signal in place — a physical fact distinct from the quiet-time window itself (S2)
The household year: holidays hosted, travel windows, school transitions
Maintenance calendar: filters, gutters, HVAC service, irrigation, chimney
Seasonal add-on windows (holiday prep, seasonal swaps) and last year's choices
Every vendor, one row each: name, scope, schedule, contact
Vendor access arrangement: how each actually gets in (verify vs stated)
Payment handling per vendor; who authorizes new work
Quality notes: the plumber they trust, the one they tolerate
Hosting style: frequency, scale, formality; guest-ready definition for THIS home
Guest room standard and turnaround
Personal touches inventory: the small things that make this family feel seen (DELIGHT rows)
The household aesthetic, in words (briefs designers, decorators, florists per WK_05H)
Catalog booking windows relevant to this home (WK_02Q: fall opens Sept 1; January resets book in December)
Gift-giving norms: budgets by relationship, wrapping standards, the go-to gifts; florist and card preferences (briefs WK_05H partners and WK_02Q bundles)

AWAY-MODE PROTOCOL: this home's travel configuration (thermostat setting, water main / water-heater vacation mode, lights cycling, plants, mail hold or collection); confirm the homeowner-insurance occupancy-check and utility requirements while away
Recurring-guests registry: who stays regularly (grandparents, siblings), their room setup, their preferences (the extra blanket, the decaf)
Observance calendar: which holidays and traditions this family actually keeps (and which they do not); drives all seasonal proposals and decor; prevents mistargeted offers
Movable-date observances (lunar/non-Gregorian): which apply, this year's dates, and their prep traditions (pre-Passover clean; clean BEFORE Lunar New Year, not during first days)
Gift & flower customs: colors, numbers, objects, and flowers to avoid; giving traditions (the gesture engine's safety check)
Rest-day / prayer-time windows: days or hours service must avoid, or prep windows service should serve (pre-sundown Friday, etc.)

COMMITMENTS LEDGER: every yes with its date and type (wedding/potluck/party/hosting/tournament); each derives its prep list (gift by T-7, dish on the order, dry cleaning by T-5, sitter by T-10); prompts at T-14 and T-3; weekend density flagged
Occupational stress calendar: employer/role-predicted load seasons (tax season, budget/shutdown season, September for teachers); register softens and service flexes on schedule

FREQUENT-TRAVELER PACK (if travel is a rhythm): go-bag contents + restock list; laundry synced to travel calendar; points/loyalty prefs; meet-and-greet / expedited-security memberships; absence coverage (house/plants/pets); return-day reset timing; jet-lag recovery prefs

OUTER-CIRCLE REGISTRY: the important people beyond the household (grandparents, godparents, close friends, key colleagues) with their dates, gift norms, and addresses; drives on-behalf-of cascades: the friend's new baby, the housewarming, and sympathy sent on the family's behalf (grief-register warmth rules apply)
Reciprocity log: gifts and kindnesses RECEIVED by the family (feeds thank-you support and next-season calibration)
EVENT CALENDAR LAYER: season schedules + on-sale/presale dates feed the commitments ledger; home vs away rhythm; tailgate hosting = a hosting commitment with derived prep
GAME-DAY / SHOW-NIGHT RITUALS: the traditions (jersey washed by Friday, the snacks, the pre-show dinner spot); protected like any tradition, honored by covering HMs per the Stranger Test

PERSON REGISTRY (feeds WK-APP-002 REL domain; generalizes the outer-circle registry): everyone the family’s care touches, name, relationship, birthday/anniversary and key dates, gift history and preferences, budget norms, sensitivity flags; drives REL-01 through REL-12 (party circuit, appreciation lists, host gifts, thank-you staging, weddings, standing commitments, delegated aging-parent cadences)
TRADITIONS REGISTRY: the rituals that make this family this family (birthday person picks dinner, first-day porch photo, Sunday pancakes); protected by covering HMs per the Stranger Test

SIGNATURE RITUAL: the ONE named, repeatable touch never skipped even on the hardest visit (the three-sentence note; the staged matcha). When time runs short, this is identity, everything else is nice-to-have

VISITING-CHILD KIT (homes without resident children): outlet covers, gate, toy drawer, booster seat, staged pre-arrival for grandchildren/young visitors, allergy-checked vs S1, fully removed after (safe Saturday, gallery Monday)
SECOND HOME open/close dates + local vendor contacts; opening provisioning list; closing winterization + away-mode + sentinel schedule
PET MILESTONE DATES (pet-primary homes): birthday / adoption (gotcha) day; marked with milestone care, sibling equity across pets, never billed
Hosting precedence for official or ceremonial functions, where relevant: seating order, introduction order, toast sequencing

EVENT RUN-OF-SHOW (built from all sections, one per milestone occasion: a birthday, an anniversary, a retirement): primary timeline with a contingency at every key risk point (weather, vendor no-show, delivery delay), decided during planning rather than improvised under pressure; a standing vendor reconfirmation at 24-48 hours out, routine practice rather than a response to a problem; vendor day-of contacts, distinct from the account manager who sold the package; setup and breakdown windows; who is responsible for each phase

POST-EVENT CHECKLIST: rental returns (what, to whom, by when), final vendor payments and gratuities, thank-you note tracking (sent/owed, addresses), lost-and-found sweep, and a short debrief note (what worked, what didn't) that feeds the next event

STANDING COMMITMENTS REGISTRY: religious or spiritual observance attendance, recurring personal appointments (therapist, trainer, standing medical/PT visits), children's extracurricular rhythm, and volunteer or community commitments that shape household timing. Day, time, who drives, and what prep the home needs before or after. Logistics only, never the reason or content, the same privacy boundary Section 1 gives medications
Extended-dormancy tier of the away-mode protocol: for a multi-week or multi-month home-country return rather than a standard trip

SOLO-COVERAGE MODE: counterpart to VISITING-CHILD MODE, for any bimodal-absence household (deployment, fire season, a tour, disaster response). A staged, reversible shift in household rhythm for the absence window and back again on return — same toggle logic applied to who is home rather than who is visiting. For a rotating-shift household, the quiet-time window is a periodically reconfirmed fact, not one set at intake and left stale

SEASONAL RESIDENCY PATTERN, for a household with a second, recurring residence: which months, which property, and whether the primary home shifts to full away-mode or a reduced home-watch scope while gone. Cross-reference the seasonal-residency membership option (WK-LEG-001) if the household is on that plan

CALL-READY BAG for an on-call, activation-eligible, or near-immediate-departure profession (medicine, reserve/Guard duty, bimodal-absence work): always ready, not restocked against a booked trip — distinct from the frequent-traveler pack
Seasonal gear rotation: where rain gear, boots, umbrellas, humidifiers, dehumidifiers, and space heaters actually live and when they move, cross-referenced to the container and label system (S15)

Emergency kit's seasonal layer: documented seasonal content swap for the standing kit (S9), not a static packing list — layers/hand warmers/blanket before winter, extra water/sun protection before summer, reviewed on the same cadence as the maintenance calendar (row 5 above)

School-closure and snow-day protocol: does the household want proactive outreach the moment a closure is announced; what extra coordination an unplanned day at home requires for this family; any on-file snow-day activities or standing preferences

DECISION HORIZON REGISTRY: major choices still being decided, not yet committed to (school admission, pediatrician or specialist search, significant renovation, home purchase, camp or program selection, wedding vendor-selection phase, elder-care placement); every real external deadline (tour windows, application and financial-aid deadlines, decision or deposit date) and the working-backward milestones toward each; extends the commitments ledger (row 23) backward from decided to deciding. STATUS field: active (working toward a deadline) or shelved (researched and paused, no external date, e.g. a pool, a provider comparison, a second child or relocation) with its own revisit trigger, a date or a condition, so real research is never lost to time
Tier and weekly rhythm per the membership agreement
In-scope for THIS home (the concrete list, not the tier brochure)
Out-of-scope and the never-do list (CAUTION rows)
Spending authority: approve-first thresholds, standing budgets, emergency authorizations (e.g., vet cap) (WK_06E discipline)
Report format this client actually reads; photo preferences (within Section 8 consent)
Escalation thresholds: call now vs note in report
Response-time expectations both directions
The quarterly review conversation: scheduled how, with whom
Add-ons this household has USED: which, when, reception
Add-ons LIKELY by rhythm: map each annual event (hosting, camps, back-to-school, holidays) to its add-on and its proposal month
Per-household proposal protocol: which add-ons to offer directly, which to route to the quarterly review, which never to raise unsolicited
Proposal framing that lands with THIS client (the "I noticed..." register); log how each proposal was received
Partner-service categories relevant to this household, each with its TIMING (now / 1-2 years / on life event)
Partner routing rules: what goes through the Operations Coordinator, what requires client conversation before any sourcing
Partner coordination consent and fee transparency: what the client has agreed Well Kept coordinates, and on what terms
Last season's add-on choices and what the family valued (feeds the October seasonal recommendation, HM recommends first)
Tier entitlements: what is included at this tier (e.g., Quarterly Household Audit included for Concierge, per WK_02Q)
Existing household service spend map: what the family already pays for monthly (the raw vendor and anticipation map)

HORIZON LIST: every foreseeable transition with its approximate date, generated from the roster (permits at 15, driver at 16, college process from 9th grade, school transitions, due seasons, aging-parent likelihood). One row per item; advance quarterly.
Horizon item 1: (event, approx date, what changes, when to raise)
Horizon item 2:
Horizon item 3:
(add horizon rows as the roster implies them)
Gesture micro-budget: standing monthly allowance for unprompted delight spending, amount set with client spending authority; HM spends without approval, logs after
Partnership neutrality noted [S2]: any sensitivities in the division-of-labor topic; the surprises exception (what is being hidden for whom, where)
Expense mechanics: company payment method (HM never uses own money), receipt-at-point-of-sale rule, reconciliation cadence, billable pass-through flagging (no markup)
Guest-and-visitor policy: may the HM have anyone present while working? (privacy + liability boundary; standard in professional household roles)
Expense & receipt mechanic: company payment method only, never personal cash; receipt captured at point of sale; reconciliation cadence; single-transaction flag threshold (per WK-HR-001 / WK-FIN-011)

Aging-parent horizon [S2]: parents' locations, living situations, and any early signals; eldercare coordination categories pre-researched (care manager, in-home care, home modification, move management); the sandwich-generation event is prepared for, never probed
Life-stage coordination notes: disability/chronic-illness logistics if relevant; end-of-life support held under the grief register rules; coordinate always, deliver never

Resident-member health horizon [S2]: any serious diagnosis or disability onset in the household; disability-logistics categories held ready (patient advocate, equipment, home-delivered therapy); log-and-wait, same warmth rules as the aging-parent signal
Retirement horizon: employment-status changes expected; the re-map prepared (always-home visit windows, office standards, travel surge); stress calendar retired with the career

MEMORY & MILESTONE REGISTER [S2]: the once-only moments a family would grieve missing (first day of school, first lost tooth, learning to ride, first communion/coming-of-age, license, prom, graduation, dorm move-in, threshold birthdays 16/18/21). Held so Well Kept quietly ensures they are marked (outfit ready, photo tradition kept, note written): delight layer, never billed

ONCE-IN-A-LIFETIME family events on the horizon: engagements/weddings, births, milestone anniversaries (25th/50th), retirement, naturalization, landmark trips, home sale/major move, empty-nest threshold. Each a coordination cascade AND a gesture moment; the hardest ones (diagnosis, death) under grief-register rules
REQUEST TRIAGE LADDER: class every inbound request emergency / urgent-same-day / routine-next-visit / someday-quarterly; committed response times by tier
DELEGATION LADDER (per domain: vendors, kids’ logistics, gestures, scheduling, purchases): DO without asking / RECOMMEND and await yes / ASK always
Proposal format preference: decision-memo style? (situation, options, recommendation, needed-by: one glance, one reply)
Expat or international-assignment horizon: a known or estimable posting end date; offboarding and relocation support pre-researched well ahead of a 60-day notice, never probed [S2]

Political-appointee horizon: a different shape of readiness than the expat horizon above — no knowable end date, an abrupt departure possible with an election, a reshuffle, or a resignation; offboarding support pre-researched the same way but triggered by the event rather than a calendar [S2]
